urn:contentItem:6JH7-9DR3-S2YK-H14N-00000-00M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - RECENT NEWS FROM DPR CONSTRUCTION AND INDUSTRY PEERS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:56:29ZLexisNexisSTOCKM. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - RECENT NEWS FROM DPR CONSTRUCTION AND INDUSTRY PEERS

DASHBOARD: M A MORTENSON

PermID5001069053Websitehttp://www.mortenson.comIndustryConstruction

ACTIVITIES:

M. A. Mortenson Companies Inc., founded in 1954 by M.A. Mortenson Sr., is a top-20 U.S. builder headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 5,000 employees across 12 offices, it delivers construction, development, and energy services, generating $5.7 billion in 2023 revenue. Mortenson specializes in healthcare, renewable energy, and data centers, with projects like Allianz Field. Its integrated services emphasize sustainability and innovation, using advanced technologies like digital modeling. Competing with Gilbane, Mortensons commitment to safety and diversity, along with its North American operations, ensures high-performing project delivery.

+ M A MORTENSON [1 News That Matters]

+ M A MORTENSON WINS $610M BIOSECURITY CONSTRUCTION PROJECT TARGETING NEW WORLD SCREWWORM

M A Mortenson Company, a Minneapolis-based construction firm, has been awarded a $610 million contract by the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers to build the Domestic New World Screwworm Sterile Insect Production Facility at Moore Airbase in Edinburg, Texas. The contract is part of a broader effort to curb the spread of New World screwworms in the United States. On March 9, 2026, officials warned of the threat if the screwworm migrates north from Mexico, with the USDA signaling urgency as cases are reported in Tamaulipas near the Texas border. As of March 4, 2026, there were 989 active cases nationwide, including 19 in Tamaulipas, up from 11 weeks earlier, illustrating the escalating risk to livestock. Officials said the project will help maintain a steady supply of sterilized flies used to disrupt the screwworm life cycle, a key component of border protection and the broader biosecurity strategy, with Mortenson coordinating construction alongside USDA efforts.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION TO GROW INNSBROOK FOOTPRINT WITH 9,100-SF EXPANSION BACKED BY HENRICO COUNTY

DPR Construction announced a 9,100-square-foot expansion of its Innsbrook office, supported by Henrico County incentives to attract and retain businesses. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion aims to accommodate a growing workforce and strengthen operational capabilities as part of the company’s regional growth strategy.The project will bring together teams and resources, promoting collaboration and innovation across the organization.Sustainable design features and modern office concepts will be integrated, aligning with DPR’s leadership in sustainable construction.Completion is expected within the next year, underscoring the company’s commitment to the region and its ongoing community initiatives.

+ JASON PAGE APPOINTED HEAD OF DPR CONSTRUCTION'S NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP

The Special Services Group handles a broad range of work, from targeted improvements like parking garage stair installations to expansive interior buildouts for new office and entertainment spaces, and Page's leadership is expected to expand the group's capabilities to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs in Nashville. Beyond project leadership, Page mentors and coaches teams, sharing best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work and fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation as DPR expands its Nashville footprint.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION SECURES GREAT PLACE TO WORK STATUS AS STAFF REPORT HIGH ENGAGEMENT

DPR Construction has secured Great Place To Work Certification, confirming that 97 percent of its employees say it is a great place to work, a figure about 40 points higher than the national average. The award, presented on 31st March 2026, signals a culture rooted in pride, belonging and opportunity across the firm’s projects.CEO George Pfeffer noted the certification reflects sentiments across the organization. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said. The company emphasizes trust, support, and growth to help employees perform at their best.DPR’s operating model empowers staff to make decisions and share information openly, encouraging initiative and cross functional collaboration on complex tasks, new roles, and ideas beyond job descriptions. A strong emphasis on learning and development supports clear career paths aligned with individual aspirations.Great Place To Work® research aligns with these findings, highlighting a sense of pride and belonging among workers. The certification has helped DPR strengthen its reputation as an employer of choice, while external accolades from U.S. News & World Report and Newsweek have recognized DPR in more than 15 U.S. cities over the last year.Founded in 1990, DPR has evolved into a multi billion dollar contractor known for complex and sustainable projects across sectors. Its innovation posture—through virtual design and construction and self performing craft—helps explain its roughly 11,000 person workforce worldwide. More information about DPR’s culture and career opportunities is available on its official website.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ TURNER CELEBRATES TOPPING OUT OF 72-MW ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER IN FREDERICK

Turner Construction Company marked the topping out of the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, signifying the completion of the structure for the 72-megawatt facility. The milestone was achieved on the 28th of April 2026, less than five months after ground had been broken. To date, the project has logged more than 80,000 craft hours, with roughly 345 skilled workers contributing to progress. The 450,000-square-foot center sits on a 75-acre campus and features a two-story structural steel design, an insulated precast exterior system, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling solution that combines liquid and air-cooled technologies to optimize performance. Turner's leadership emphasized that topping out represents more than erecting the frame, underscoring the strength of the partnership with Aligned and the team’s commitment to safe, efficient delivery. As the project moves into interior fit-out and operational readiness, stakeholders will watch closely, anticipating the facility’s role in supporting the region’s digital infrastructure.

+ GEISINGER WYOMING VALLEY EXPANSION MOVES TO VERTICAL CONSTRUCTION AS TURNER BREAKS GROUND

WILKES-BARRE, PA — Turner Construction has advanced to vertical construction on Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center's $900 million expansion, moving forward after years of planning and foundation work. Steel now rises on an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower that will lift beds from 356 to 500 and expand operating rooms from 12 to 18. Officials say the addition will enlarge the emergency department and ensure all patient rooms are private, with completion expected in the first quarter of 2028. The date of this announcement is 28th April 2026.David Kaminski, Turner’s Vice President and Senior General Manager, described the milestone as a key step toward broader health care access for families in northeastern Pennsylvania and a chance to partner with Geisinger on a project that strengthens the community for decades. The project is projected to create as many as 3,800 construction jobs and generate about $1 billion in local economic impact. Onsite employment is expected to rise from 150 workers to as many as 450 during peak activity, with Geisinger planning to hire an additional 400 staff after opening.Geisinger Wyoming Valley's campus opened in 1981 and has since expanded to meet rising demand for medical services. The new tower will bring advanced capabilities comparable to those typically found in major academic medical centers serving large metropolitan areas.

+ JMA VENTURES NAMES TURNER CONSTRUCTION LEAD ON WESTCOURT ORLANDO DEVELOPMENT

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, a development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a milestone in the project’s progression. The decision to partner with Turner stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, said that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, including coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches on Florida’s Space Coast. He recalled an early meeting in which he told the team, "If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us." Turner has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, handling pre-construction with JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Early site activities such as utility mapping and surveying are underway to ensure a smooth transition into the next phases. The Westcourt project is valued at approximately $500 million and will include a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center, with peak on-site employment around 800 workers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN LAUNCHES FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ACCELERATE CONSTRUCTION MANUFACTURING

JE Dunn Construction has launched Form Off-Site Solutions, its off-site manufacturing initiative focused on Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex construction components. The program, announced with a launch date of 6th April 2026, builds on the company’s century-long Kansas City heritage while extending capabilities nationwide.Form Off-Site Solutions structures its offerings into four product segments: metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies, designed to meet the varied needs of owners, general contractors, trade partners, and original equipment manufacturers.The end-to-end service model covers estimating, design, manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, all aimed at improving installation efficiency, safety, and quality, while providing greater cost certainty and helping shorten project timelines amid labor shortages.Off-Site Manufacturing Director Nick Effenheim emphasizes collaboration: "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and focus on getting their ideas, concepts, and design into a manufactured environment is something that sets us apart."JE Dunn President and CEO Gordon Lansford says the Form launch marks a major step in the firm’s ongoing push to drive innovation for clients as Form’s production facilities in Kansas City and Nashville position the unit to serve a national client base.

+ STEEL BEAM PLACED AS NORTHLAND CENTER REACHES TOPPING-OUT MILESTONE

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — The topping-out of the Northland Workforce Development Center was celebrated on 19th March 2026, as JE Dunn Construction placed the final structural steel beam for the 145,000-square-foot, two-story facility.Before the beam was hoisted into place, a beam-signing ceremony brought together the Platte County R-3 School District, partner districts, project stakeholders, and community members who signed the beam bearing project and partner logos. Brad Hokanson, Project Executive at JE Dunn Construction, said, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The center is being built to house programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services, expanding capacity for Northland Career Center and partner institutions while serving both high school students and adult learners seeking credentials. Planned spaces include flexible labs, classrooms, hands-on training environments, and technology-rich areas that reflect real-world workplaces.Located in the Northland region of Kansas City, the project is progressing on schedule, with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening expected after the milestone. Executive Director Brian Noller of Platte County School District noted that state, local government, business, investor, and community foundation support have enabled a long-term investment in students and the region.JE Dunn is serving as construction manager, working in partnership with the school district and Clark & Enersen, with a focus on safety, schedule adherence, and coordination while maintaining open communication with stakeholders and minimizing disruption to nearby facilities.

+ JE DUNN REINSTATES CHRIS CHELETTE AS SENIOR VP, HEALTHCARE, TO EXPAND REGIONAL PRESENCE

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025. During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region. In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction. Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively. As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GILBANE [3 News That Matters]

+ ANDREAS VLAHAKIS APPOINTED TO LEAD CAPITAL MARKETS AT GILBANE DEVELOPMENT

As of 28th April 2026, Gilbane Development has named Andreas Vlahakis as head of capital markets, to operate from the firm's New York City office. He brings extensive experience in real estate capital markets across asset classes, with a focus on ground-up multifamily and student housing investments.In this role, Vlahakis will refine and execute Gilbane's funding strategy, report to James Patchett, president and CEO, and help shape the capital strategy, drive investment decisions, and nurture relationships with funding and equity partners. He will also lead transaction structuring and negotiations to position Gilbane to capitalize on evolving market opportunities.Previously, Vlahakis served as managing director for capital markets at Thor Equities, leading equity-raising efforts for a range of projects and partnerships. His prior role as senior vice president and chief of staff at Thor Equities underscores his broad financial leadership as the firm pursues growth.Gilbane Development says the appointment aligns with its commitment to quality and innovation and positions the firm for growth by strengthening its capital discipline and investment program.Investors will likely be watching how Vlahakis's background translates into stronger capital relationships and execution in Gilbane's future real estate deals.

+ WKU FORMS GILBANE ALLIANCE TO DEVELOP NEW RESIDENCE HALL AND UPGRADES

The Western Kentucky University Student Government Association announced the formal approval of WKU's public-private partnership with Gilbane, designed to expand housing and campus facilities. The agreement was approved by WKU's Board of Regents on 11th December 2025, with Gilbane tasked to design and build a new residence hall to replace Douglas Keen and Hugh Poland Halls. With the Kentucky Legislature's approval on 6th April 2026, construction is authorized to commence. SGA Senator Jakob Barker confirmed that the partnership with Gilbane is fully authorized and stated, "WKU is now in the clear to start building the new residence halls." Beyond housing, the SGA advanced several measures aimed at improving student resources. Resolution 7-26-S backs installing a crosswalk at a key intersection near housing and urges collaboration with the Kentucky Department of Transportation to address student safety. Bill 17-26-S creates the Textbooks for Toppers program, which will provide $50 reimbursements for required course materials. Additional bills will supply essential academic items such as Scantrons and lab goggles to reduce out-of-pocket costs for students. Taken together, the developments reflect WKU's broader strategy to enhance the student experience through strategic housing development and targeted support for student resources.

+ PHASE II LEASING OPENS FOR GILBANE’S $135M BARNABY & 7TH DEVELOPMENT IN DC

Gilbane Development has moved Phase II of the Barnaby & 7th project into leasing, adding 229 units in four new buildings: The Stoneview, The Solomon, The Marte, and The Lewis in Southeast Washington, D.C. As of 3rd April 2026, Phase II is scheduled for completion by summer 2026 and provides a mix of studio to three-bedroom units designed for households earning 50 percent and 80 percent of AMI. Twenty percent of the apartments will be Permanent Supportive Housing units, managed by the District's Department of Homeless Services.Phase I, The Upland, was finished in Spring 2025 and contains 169 apartments, including 34 PSH units. Income limits for Phase I range from 30 to 50 percent of AMI.The third phase is planned to add roughly 100 more rental units in a single building, bringing the project total to about 498 units upon completion.During Phase II construction, a new pedestrian walkway was built to connect all six buildings within the larger development. The District’s Housing Finance Agency estimates Phase II cost at about $135 million, illustrating Gilbane's commitment to affordable housing and enhanced community connectivity in Washington, D.C.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP APPOINTS CARLY REINER CACERES AS NATIONAL CAMPUS RECRUITING HR DIRECTOR

Clark Construction Group appointed Carly Reiner Caceres as Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting, effective Apr. 29, 2026, with a mandate to advance national campus recruiting initiatives and strengthen early-career talent pipelines for its broad portfolio of projects.Caceres arrives from Proper, where she served as Vice President of People Operations, shaping HR strategy and operations in a hybrid workplace. Her background also includes leadership roles at MILLER as Director of Internal Operations and more than nine years at Accenture as Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Her prior experience at Grant Thornton LLP overseeing campus recruiting strategies further reinforces her capability to build robust internship programs and solid student engagement initiatives.Clark Construction Group, established in 1906, has a long track record across education and infrastructure markets. The company says Caceres will bring strategic HR expertise to attract top graduates and mentor the next generation of construction professionals.Her early career featured internships at Charleston Magazine and the Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum, rounding out a diverse background in communications and external relations that will help guide talent development efforts.

+ RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL AWARDS MULTI-TRADES FRAMEWORK CONTRACT TO CLARK CONTRACTS FOR DOMESTIC HOUSING WORKS

Clark Contracts has been appointed to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades contractors framework to improve delivery of repair, maintenance and refurbishment services for domestic properties across the authority. The appointment was announced on 11th February 2026 and the framework runs through 2027.The framework covers a broad range of multi-trade works, enabling the council to manage both responsive and planned maintenance more effectively across its housing stock and ensuring residents benefit from a faster, more reliable service.Clark Contracts is a well-established public-sector supplier, with current agreements including Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework. The company also participates in the Crown Commercial Service’s Construction Framework and several Scottish Procurement Alliance frameworks.Gordon Cunningham, managing director at Clark Contracts, said the appointment builds on a track record of more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over the past three years and demonstrates the firm’s commitment to serving local communities.The deal supports Clark Contracts’ growing public-sector footprint as it works with councils and institutions such as the University of Glasgow and Glasgow City Council to deliver essential housing services.

+ CDC APPROVES AMENDMENT FOR ₱4-B THE VILLAGES DEVELOPMENT, PKR JOINS CLARK GROWTH DRIVE

Clark Freeport is set to host a ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages after Clark Development Corp. approved an amendment to PKR Corp.'s lease on December 13, 2025. The revised lease provides PKR with a 30-month window to complete construction and includes safeguards aimed at ensuring on-time delivery. Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera, CDC president and CEO, noted that the amendment reinforces investor confidence and the efficient execution of projects, while keeping The Villages as a community hub where residents can live and participate. PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung thanked CDC for the partnership and said the company will deliver the Clark project, with PKR operating under Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for safety, technology, and workplace practices. The initiative fits CDC's broader push to accelerate investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors.

value.Title: Bill reauthorizing Public Utilities Commission races through state Capitol. The story is what's not included.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJT-TN43-S5W7-X3X2-00000-00Bill reauthorizing Public Utilities Commission races through state Capitol. The story is what's not included.2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:44:04ZLexisNexisBUSINESSBill reauthorizing Public Utilities Commission races through state Capitol. The story is what's not included.Mark Jaffe

Facing rising utility bills, clean energy mandates and new demands for power, especially from data centers, a chorus of utilities and business groups is united saying the Colorado Public Utilities Commission needs an overhaul.

In part, the push appears to be prompted by some controversial commission decisions. On Saturday, Republican lawmakers tried unsuccessfully to amend the bill dealing with the routine reauthorization of the commission to expand the three-person panel to five members.

"We're simply not getting a good representation on the PUC with three people," said Rep. Dan Woog, a Frederick Republican. "It's just of the utmost importance that we have people there that understand different parts of the state."

Xcel Energy, which is regulated by the PUC and has $511 million in electricity and gas rate increases pending before the commission, has been a major advocate of a five-member commission.

Robert Kenney, president of Xcel's Colorado subsidiary, said it would be an opportunity to add "more ideological diversity" to the commission.

"Being able to test your ideas against other people who don't necessarily share your same perspective leads to generally better public policy outcomes," Kenney said. "So that's what I mean about greater ideological diversity."

"It is not a commentary on the three specific commissioners. I will be really clear about that," Kenney said.

A compromise amendment was added for a study of the composition, budget and operations of the PUC. The fiscal note on a bill for a five-member commission last session estimated the cost at just under $1 million.

House Biill 1326, also known as the Sunset Public Utilities Commission bill, is racing through the legislative session in its final days and would extend the operations of the PUC for 11 years.

What does the Public Utilities Commission oversee?

The commission oversees 178 companies and services ranging from taxis to telecommunications to railroads to pipelines.

Its main focus, however, has been on the electric and gas utilities it regulates and particularly the single biggest utility, the subsidiary of Minneapolis-based Xcel Energy, which serves 1.6 million electric customers and 1.5 million natural gas customers in the state.

Some critics contend that the commission is putting green energy ahead of cost savings; others say that it is focused on urban areas at the expense of rural ones. Xcel Energy has criticized the size of some of its rate and resource decisions.

"The PUC has been performing well in its role of protecting consumers from potential exploitation from monopoly utilities," said Erin Overturf, clean energy director for the environmental nonprofit Western Resource Advocates.

"So, it is interesting that we're seeing what kind of feels like a coordinated effort from utilities to undermine faith in the PUC or to fundamentally reshape the agency."

It is an unprecedented time for the commission as the rate requests and investment plans rise into the billions of dollars, the legislature continues to issue mandates to the PUC and the demand for power rises.

"We're dealing with the largest demand for energy probably since the invention of air conditioning," said Ray Rivera, director of Colorado Energy Crossroads, a coalition of labor unions, business groups and utilities.

"So, you know, we need to modernize the PUC to meet that demand," Rivera said. The two electric utilities regulated by the PUC Xcel Energy and Black Hills Energy are coalition members.

There have been a few decisions that have drawn criticism and huge spending requests have sometimes left utilities and the commission at loggerheads.

A commission ruling, employing a little-used statute, overturned an Elbert County denial of Xcel Energy permits for a high-tension transmission line through the county, sparking anger from rural legislators.

The decision allowed Xcel Energy to use eminent domain to gain the rights of way it needs. Elbert County has asked for a rehearing before the PUC.

The commission has received a steady stream of mandates from the legislature, which in the last five years passed at least 20 bills to initiate programs to do things such as promote EVs, mitigate wildfires, upgrade distribution lines and cut greenhouse gas emissions.

Legislation enabled the utilities to do things like add charges on customer bills for new programs or allow Xcel Energy to accelerate recovery of funds or include performance bonuses.

The Xcel Energy EV, wildfire mitigation, distribution and clean heat plans to reduce greenhouse gas emissions from natural gas utilities total about $3 billion.

In each case the commission was left to interpret the legislature's orders and devise a plan.

For the 2019 "Just Transition from Coal-based Electrical Energy Economy" law, the commission had to come up with ways to soften the blow of coal-fired plants closing in Craig, Hayden, Pueblo and Morgan County.

The solution, however, left some of the communities, especially Pueblo, feeling shortchanged. Pueblo business organizations support the five-member commission now.

"The legislature has the lion's share of the blame here, because they are handing the PUC increasingly incoherent mandates," said Sarah Montalbano, an analyst with the Independence Institute, a conservative political nonprofit.

"They're doing a fair job with what they're being handed by the legislature, but they've absolutely been straying from ratepayer protection," Montalbano said.

Particularly contentious was the commission's execution of Senate Bill 264, the 2021 legislation requiring natural gas utilities to develop "Clean Heat Plans" to cut their greenhouse gas emissions 22% by 2030 with a 2.5% cap on how much rates could rise.

The utilities presented data to the commission that they couldn't get to a 22% reduction with the funds limited by a 2.5% rate cap. The commission granted waivers. Xcel Energy's Clean Heat Plan will cost $440 million.

"I think they strayed from the cost protection mission on that one," Montalbano said.

And then in December the commission voted to raise the 2035 reduction target to 41%. Xcel Energy proposed 30% and environmental groups were pushing for 55%. It was a 2-1 vote, with PUC Chairman Eric Blank voicing concern about the sharp increase.

Advance Colorado, which describes itself as an organization focused on "reversing radical policies that are harming the state," has filed a ballot initiative to put "a right to natural gas" in the state constitution.

Commissioners claim shortage of information

As the demands for new investment and rates have increased and Xcel Energy has faced problems running its plants, the commissioners have expressed frustration with the company.

In 2024, the commission was deluged with complaints as Xcel customers suffered a doubling of blackouts and logged customer complaints jumped 100% in three years, according to a commission report.

"We're seeing a company before us requesting tens of billions of dollars to buy new things, to do new things when they appear to be falling short of their basic core commitments to the customers that they exist to serve," Commissioner Megan Gilman said at the time.

Xcel Energy responded to the report, saying it was adding staff and addressing the issues.

The commission has also repeatedly chided Xcel for not providing adequate information for its decision-making, most recently citing issues with transmission.

"I am really frustrated with the company's transmission analysis and planning," Blank said May 6. "They need to quickly provide us with a better picture of the transmission system … before we spend hundreds of millions of dollars or billions of dollars in additional resources."

The commission has also been concerned over multiple plant failures potentially leading to a shortage of peak power for the summer.

In August, the breakdown-prone Comanche 3 Unit in Pueblo was damaged by "severe vibrations." In late November, Hayden Units 1 and 2 went offline due to a partial collapse of a portion of a scrubber removing pollutants from emissions.

Xcel's Cabin Creek Pump Storage Project and its 93 megawatts of power suffered a turbine failure in February.

"I've been on this commission for six years," Gilman said at one review. "This is the second entire year that Comanche 3 has not run. That's a third of the time I've been here, and that's only with these two major outages."

However, the prospect of billions of dollars in investments driving up rates concerns the commission most.

Xcel Energy, as all investor-owned utilities, makes its money not so much by selling electricity as building new plants and wires, putting them into a so-called rate base, and getting that money back, plus a return on it.

The utility's $15 billion, four-year investment plan for its electric system would by one estimate increase the rate base to about $38 billion in 2032. It was $6 billion in 2021.

This on its own could lead to a doubling or tripling of electricity rates.

Xcel Energy says the investments are needed to upgrade an aging grid, provide for growing electricity demand, harden the system against wildfire and extreme weather, ensure reliable service and meet the state clean energy goals.

The investments and rates will be offset by a big increase in electricity sales to data centers and other industrial users, the company projects.

The commissioners say they are concerned about what happens if the capital investments are made and the sales and revenue growth don't materialize.

"It appears increasingly that the benefits are set to flow to the company given any scenario and the vast majority if not all the risk appears to be on the customer. That is the piece we need to address," Gilman said at one meeting.

Republicans argue for geographic representation

Xcel Energy had sought 12,000 to 14,000 MW of new generation in its plan, but concerned about the impact on rates, the PUC scaled it back to about 7,000 MW.

"This is a regulated monopoly operating in a legally defined service territory where competition is prohibited, and in return for that privilege of operating as a monopoly, they shouldn't be driving profit at the expense of customers," Blank said previously.

Xcel Energy is seeking a $356 million electricity rate hike and a $155 million natural gas rate increase. The commission staff calculates that the electricity increase would raise residential bills 30%.

In 2025, Xcel Energy's Colorado operations made $903 million in operating profits.

It is against this backdrop that the debate on overhauling the commission is taking place.

"We're all trying to do the same thing on behalf of our customers in our communities," Xcel Energy's Kenney said. "There's been an increasing or evolving tension, and I think that's unfortunate."

There is a hope that expanding the commission could defuse some of that tension.

"How can we create something that's not adversarial, but rather helping look at long-term planning, look at alignment?" said Richard Werner, Upstate Colorado Economic Development CEO and a supporter of a five-member commission.

Of course, since the PUC is a quasi-judicial process, it is designed in some measure to be adversarial.

The Republican legislators offering amendments were pushing for geographic representation.

"It's common sense certainly for those of us who believe that we need to do this," Woog said. "The PUC wields a lot of power to serve the entire state," Woog said.

Blank is a Boulder resident. Gilman lives in Edwards and Commissioner Tom Plant is from Buena Vista.

The Public Utilities Commission was created in 1913 and proponents of the five-member board say it is time for a change.

"The world in which we are operating today is infinitely more complex than it was, certainly 100 years ago and even 10 years ago, which, to my mind, dismisses the argument of continuing to do things the same way," Kenney said.

Though, he did say the majority of utility commissions in the U.S. have three members.

"I don't completely understand the focus on fundamentally restructuring the commission right now," Overturf, of Western Resource Advocates, said. "It is not necessarily going to result in increased efficiency or better outcomes. In fact, they're just going to raise the costs associated with running the agency.

"It looks like a solution in search of a problem."

urn:contentItem:6JK2-7YM3-RS4R-212F-00000-00M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:56:28ZLexisNexisSTOCKM. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - THE RECENT IN THE SECTOR

DASHBOARD: M A MORTENSON

PermID5001069053Websitehttp://www.mortenson.comIndustryConstruction

ACTIVITIES:

M. A. Mortenson Companies Inc., founded in 1954 by M.A. Mortenson Sr., is a top-20 U.S. builder headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 5,000 employees across 12 offices, it delivers construction, development, and energy services, generating $5.7 billion in 2023 revenue. Mortenson specializes in healthcare, renewable energy, and data centers, with projects like Allianz Field. Its integrated services emphasize sustainability and innovation, using advanced technologies like digital modeling. Competing with Gilbane, Mortensons commitment to safety and diversity, along with its North American operations, ensures high-performing project delivery.

+ M A MORTENSON [1 News That Matters]

+ M A MORTENSON SECURES $610M CONTRACT FOR INNOVATIVE SCREWWORM FACILITY

M A Mortenson Company, a Minneapolis-based construction firm, has been awarded a substantial $610 million contract to construct a facility aimed at combating the spread of New World screwworms. The announcement was made by the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers as part of the ongoing efforts to protect U.S. livestock from this invasive pest. The contract will fund the development of the Domestic New World Screwworm Sterile Insect Production Facility at Moore Airbase in Edinburg, Texas.On the 9th of March 2026, it was noted that the New World screwworm poses a significant threat to livestock in the United States if it migrates north from Mexico. The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) has identified the urgency of the situation, particularly as active cases of the pest have been reported in Tamaulipas, a northern Mexican state bordering Texas. Recent reports indicate an increase in active cases in this region, despite an overall decline in reported cases across Mexico.As of the 4th of March 2026, a total of 989 active cases of New World screwworm were documented, with 19 of these cases located in Tamaulipas, an alarming rise from 11 cases reported just weeks prior. This escalation has raised concerns among agricultural stakeholders in both Mexico and the U.S., especially given that New World screwworms can cause severe damage to livestock by laying larvae in animal wounds, potentially resulting in death within two weeks if left untreated.In response to the growing threat, health officials have initiated several preventive measures, including the deployment of sterilized flies along the U.S.-Mexico border to disrupt the breeding cycle of the screwworms. M A Mortenson's role in constructing this facility will be pivotal in ensuring a consistent supply of these sterilized flies, which are crucial for controlling the pest population and protecting livestock in the border regions.The construction of the sterile insect production facility is not only a significant investment in agricultural biosecurity but also reflects broader efforts to safeguard the livestock industry, especially in states with high agricultural outputs. With increasing collaboration between the USDA and construction firms like M A Mortenson, stakeholders hope to mitigate the risks posed by the New World screwworm and secure the livelihoods of ranchers and farmers alike.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION EXPANDS INNSBROOK OFFICE WITH LOCAL SUPPORT

DPR Construction, a leading regional construction firm, is set to expand its office space in Innsbrook by 9,100 square feet. The announcement comes as part of a strategic move to accommodate the company's growing workforce and enhance its operational capabilities. The expansion is supported by local incentives from Henrico County, which aims to attract and retain businesses in the area. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The new office space will allow DPR Construction to bring together teams and resources, fostering collaboration and innovation within the company. This growth is expected to create additional job opportunities in the region, further contributing to Henrico's economic development goals. Local officials have expressed optimism about the project, viewing it as a significant investment in the community's future.DPR Construction has been a prominent player in the construction industry, known for its commitment to quality and sustainability. The company's decision to expand its presence in Innsbrook reflects its confidence in the local market and the potential for continued growth. The support from Henrico County is part of broader efforts to enhance the business environment, making it easier for companies to invest and thrive.As part of the expansion, DPR Construction plans to implement modern design features and sustainable practices in the new office layout. This aligns with the company's mission to lead in sustainable construction practices while also providing a conducive work environment for its employees. The expansion is anticipated to be completed within the next year, further solidifying DPR Construction's commitment to the region.In addition to the expansion, DPR Construction has been actively involved in various community initiatives, underscoring its dedication to corporate social responsibility. As the company prepares for this new chapter, stakeholders are keenly watching how this expansion will impact both the firm and the local economy.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION APPOINTS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP

DPR Construction has announced the appointment of Jason Page as the new lead for its Special Services Group in Nashville. As of 1st April 2026, Page brings over 14 years of experience to the role, having joined DPR Construction in January 2012. His career has been marked by a versatile portfolio, encompassing projects from minor renovations to large-scale, complex developments.Since relocating to Nashville in April 2021, Jason Page has successfully spearheaded numerous corporate interior and entertainment projects, consistently delivering high-quality results under tight deadlines. His approach emphasizes a client-first leadership style, which has allowed him to cultivate strong relationships across various market sectors in the region. Page's passion for renovation and tenant improvement work has positioned him as a key player in DPR Construction's local operations.The DPR Special Services Group (SSG) plays a pivotal role in executing a diverse range of projects, from targeted improvements like parking garage stair installations to expansive interior buildouts for new office and entertainment spaces. Under Page's leadership, the SSG is expected to enhance its capabilities in meeting the evolving needs of clients and stakeholders in Nashville.In addition to project leadership, Jason Page has been actively involved in mentoring and coaching project teams within DPR Construction. He has shared best practices for executing quick-turn, high-impact work, fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation. His dedication to developing talent within the organization is seen as a valuable asset as DPR Construction continues to expand its footprint in Nashville.With Jason Page at the helm of the Special Services Group, DPR Construction is well-positioned to leverage his expertise and leadership as it navigates the competitive landscape of Nashville's construction market. The company's commitment to delivering exceptional service and quality projects remains steadfast, supported by Page's extensive experience and strategic vision.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK® CERTIFICATION AMID HIGH EMPLOYEE SATISFACTION

DPR Construction, a leader in the technical building sector, has secured Great Place To Work® Certification, with an impressive 97 percent of its employees affirming that it is a great place to work. This remarkable statistic is notably 40 points higher than the typical score given to workplaces across the United States. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, underscores DPR's commitment to fostering a workplace culture characterized by pride, belonging, and opportunity.George Pfeffer, CEO of DPR Construction, emphasized the significance of this certification, which reflects the sentiments of the employees who embody the company's culture. He stated, "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life." Pfeffer highlighted the company's focus on creating an inclusive environment where employees feel trusted, supported, and encouraged to grow, thus enabling them to perform at their best.DPR Construction's operational philosophy is built on empowering employees across its various projects to make decisions and share information openly. This approach encourages initiative, whether it involves innovating on complex tasks, stepping into new roles, or contributing ideas beyond formal job descriptions. Employees benefit from career paths aligned with their aspirations, supported by various learning and development programs.The research conducted by Great Place To Work® corroborates DPR's employee-centric strategy, revealing that a significant portion of the workforce feels a sense of pride in their contributions and a strong sense of belonging within the company. The certification further enhances DPR's reputation as an employer of choice, with additional accolades from U.S. News & World Report and Newsweek recognizing it as one of the best companies to work for, particularly in over 15 U.S. cities over the past year.DPR Construction has built a strong legacy since its founding in 1990, evolving into a multi-billion-dollar organization that specializes in complex and sustainable projects across various sectors. Its commitment to innovation, through the use of virtual design and construction and self-performing craft, positions DPR among the top contractors in the industry, employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide. For more details about DPR's culture and career opportunities, visit their official website.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION UNVEILS FREE AI TOOL FOR ENHANCED JOBSITE SAFETY

Turner Construction has launched SafeT Coach, a free AI-powered tool designed to enhance safety on construction job sites. This innovative solution is accessible from any mobile device and aims to facilitate real-time discussions centered around safety protocols. On 9th May 2026, the company highlighted that SafeT Coach answers safety-related questions using Turner’s Environmental Health & Safety standards, rather than generic sources available on the internet.The tool is engineered to foster a coaching mindset among supervisors and craft professionals, focusing on dialogue about safer work methods rather than mere enforcement of rules. Outputs generated by SafeT Coach are framed as potential hazards, supporting the professional judgment of users. The development of this tool involved collaboration with over 80 stakeholders through design exercises and field pilots, ensuring its relevance and effectiveness.Since its pilot introduction, SafeT Coach has recorded more than 25,000 interactions involving Turner staff, trade partners, and field teams. For instance, Darren Dreas, a superintendent working on a higher-education lab project, utilized the tool to evaluate whether a vertical shaft met the criteria for a permit-required confined space. With SafeT Coach, he was able to quickly access a decision flow chart, a start-of-day permit checklist, and relevant policy citations, facilitating an informed discussion with a trade partner’s safety manager.Dreas commented on the efficiency of the tool, stating, “Using SafeT Coach saved hours of research and analysis, helping us to quickly make informed decisions.” This positive feedback underscores the potential impact of SafeT Coach on improving safety protocols in the construction industry, enabling better decision-making on-site and promoting a culture of safety.As construction continues to evolve with technology integration, Turner Construction's introduction of SafeT Coach represents a significant step towards leveraging AI for practical safety solutions. The tool's free accessibility opens opportunities for various companies across the industry to enhance their safety measures without incurring additional costs, potentially leading to broader adoption of advanced safety practices.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION SELECTED FOR MAJOR $500 MILLION WESTCOURT PROJECT

Turner Construction Company has been selected to serve as the general contractor for the ambitious Westcourt project, a $500 million mixed-use development located adjacent to the Kia Center in downtown Orlando. This announcement was made on 5th May 2026, marking a significant step forward for a project that has faced multiple delays in recent years.The Westcourt development is planned to cover approximately 8.5 acres and will feature a sports and entertainment district ranging from 900,000 to 1 million square feet. Key components of the project include a 265-key Kimpton-branded hotel, 269 high-rise residential units, and up to 310,000 square feet of Class A office space. Additionally, the plan encompasses over 125,000 square feet dedicated to dining and entertainment, a 3,500-capacity venue for live events, and 1,140 on-site parking spaces.Turner Construction has indicated that during peak construction periods, the project will support around 800 workers on-site and is expected to create approximately 1,600 direct construction jobs throughout its duration. The firm anticipates engaging many trade partners from the Central Florida area, which could bolster local employment opportunities.In April 2024, the project received necessary land use entitlements, with the Kimpton hotel partnership announced later that same year, marking the brand's inaugural location in Orlando. Public support has also been secured, with Orlando’s Community Redevelopment Agency committing $2.5 million to the project, potentially reimbursing the developer up to $40 million over time through future property tax growth generated by Westcourt.Looking ahead, development documents suggest a targeted groundbreaking in the second quarter of 2026, with the projected opening set for late 2028. While construction cranes are not expected to rise immediately, the attachment of a reputable builder like Turner Construction brings renewed momentum to one of downtown Orlando's most anticipated projects.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION EXPANDS INDIAN OPERATIONS WITH NEW WEWORK WORKSPACE

Turner Construction is solidifying its presence in India with the establishment of a new managed workspace in Airoli, Mumbai. This expansion marks a significant milestone for the global construction firm as it partners with WeWork India. The new facility, which spans 46,000 square feet and accommodates over 650 professionals, was opened on the 7th of May, 2026, reflecting Turner’s long-term commitment to India's infrastructure and talent ecosystem.The decision by Turner Construction to consolidate its teams in a large-scale managed office underscores the growing recognition among enterprises that workspace design can be a strategic enabler of collaboration and operational agility. Exclusively designed for Turner, this office aligns with the company's global standards while leveraging WeWork India's design capabilities. Jairam Panch, Chief Operating Officer of Turner Construction, stated that the new workspace is more than just a physical location; it embodies the company's investment in an environment that fosters collaboration, creativity, and excellence.Turner’s relationship with WeWork India began in 2022 with a modest 16-seat private office, which has now evolved into a comprehensive enterprise-grade managed office solution. As Turner's operations in India expanded, the need for a flexible workspace that supports employee convenience and corporate identity became paramount. Airoli was chosen for its connectivity, talent access, and burgeoning business ecosystem. The managed office incorporates Turner’s precision-focused approach along with WeWork India's design thinking, featuring collaborative spaces and modern amenities.The rise in demand for managed offices like the one established by Turner Construction reflects a broader shift in enterprise perspectives on workspaces. As noted by Santosh Martin, Chief Revenue Officer of WeWork India, companies are increasingly viewing their work environments not just as operational assets, but as critical drivers of organizational performance and culture. Turner’s transition from a small office to a larger, customized workspace exemplifies the trend towards flexible models that facilitate growth while maintaining a strong corporate identity.With this latest addition, WeWork India continues to expand its footprint among enterprise clients, with 76 percent of its member base in Mumbai representing various sectors, including technology and finance. Operating across eight cities with over 100,000 desks, WeWork India is positioning flexible workspaces as an essential component of modern business infrastructure in the country.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION UNVEILS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ENHANCE MANUFACTURING CAPABILITIES

JE Dunn Construction has launched its new off-site manufacturing initiative, Form Off-Site Solutions(TradeMark), designed to specialize in Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex construction components. This strategic move comes as the company seeks to capitalize on its century-long legacy in Kansas City and beyond. The introduction of Form Off-Site Solutions is positioned as a response to the growing demand for advanced manufacturing capabilities, especially in a rapidly evolving construction landscape. The launch date of this initiative is 6th April 2026.Led by Off-Site Manufacturing Director Nick Effenheim, Form Off-Site Solutions brings together skilled craftspeople, advanced automation, and innovative strategies to design and produce specialized components for a diverse array of stakeholders, including owners, general contractors, trade partners, and original equipment manufacturers. The offerings are categorized into four distinct product segments: metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies, ensuring that various market needs are met with tailored solutions.The end-to-end service model of Form Off-Site Solutions addresses the multifaceted challenges inherent in construction projects. By providing capabilities that range from estimating and design to manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, JE Dunn Construction aims to deliver improved installation efficiency, enhanced project safety and quality, and maximized cost certainty. This comprehensive approach is intended to alleviate labor shortages and accelerate project timelines, critical factors in today's construction environment.Effenheim emphasizes the collaborative nature of Form's operations, stating, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and focus on getting their ideas, concepts, and design into a manufactured environment is something that sets us apart." This commitment to collaboration and innovation is echoed by JE Dunn President and CEO Gordon Lansford, who notes that the development of Form Off-Site Solutions represents a significant advancement in the company's ongoing quest to drive innovation for its clients.With production facilities located in Kansas City and Nashville, Form Off-Site Solutions is poised to serve clients on a national scale. The company's focus on innovation and strategic thinking is a cornerstone of its operations, aimed at enhancing project safety and quality while also addressing labor shortages in the industry. As JE Dunn Construction continues to push the boundaries of off-site manufacturing, the launch of Form Off-Site Solutions marks a pivotal step in its mission to deliver projects with greater efficiency and certainty.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION CELEBRATES TOPPING-OUT MILESTONE FOR WORKFORCE CENTER

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — Construction has reached a key milestone on the Northland Workforce Development Center, as JE Dunn Construction and project partners recently celebrated the topping out of the new facility. The event, held on 19th March 2026, marked the placement of the final structural steel beam for the project, signaling significant progress on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story building designed to expand access to career and technical education across the Northland region.Prior to the beam’s installation, attendees participated in a traditional beam-signing ceremony, adding their names and messages to a steel beam bearing project and partner logos. This moment underscored the collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner school districts, project stakeholders, and the broader community in advancing a shared workforce development vision. Brad Hokanson, Project Executive at JE Dunn Construction, emphasized the importance of this collaboration, stating, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The Northland Workforce Development Center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services. Once complete, the facility will expand the capacity of Northland Career Center and its partners, serving both high school students and adult learners seeking industry-aligned training and credentials. Planned program spaces include flexible labs and classrooms, hands-on training environments, and technology-rich areas that reflect real-world workplace conditions.Located in the Northland region of Kansas City, the project is progressing on schedule, with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated following the structural milestone. Brian Noller, Executive Director of Business Services and NWDC Campaign Lead at Platte County School District, highlighted the collaborative efforts, stating, 'With support from the State of Missouri, local municipalities, businesses and industries, individual investors, and community foundations, we are making a long-term investment in the success of our students and the future of our region.'JE Dunn is serving as construction manager for the project, working in partnership with the school district and design firm Clark & Enersen. The project team has emphasized safety, schedule adherence, and coordination throughout construction, while maintaining communication with stakeholders and minimizing disruption to nearby facilities. The Northland Workforce Development Center represents a regional investment in workforce readiness, aligning educational infrastructure with evolving industry needs and supporting long-term economic development in the Kansas City area.

+ CHRIS CHELETTE REJOINS JE DUNN TO BOLSTER HEALTHCARE SECTOR

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025.During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region.In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction.Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively.As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

+ GILBANE [3 News That Matters]

+ GILBANE BUILDING CHAMPIONS SAFETY AT NATIONAL MALL EVENT WITH OSHA ALLIANCE

WASHINGTON — Nearly 1,000 construction industry professionals gathered on the National Mall this week as Gilbane Building, chair of Construction Safety Week 2026, led a signature event highlighting worker protection and the formal signing of a new alliance with the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA). The event took place on the 12th of May 2026 and also marked the 13th annual National Safety Stand-Down to Prevent Falls in Construction, a nationwide initiative focused on reducing fall-related injuries and fatalities in the industry.The newly announced alliance between Construction Safety Week and OSHA is aimed at strengthening collaboration between industry and government to prevent serious injuries and fatalities while advancing 'Total Worker Health' initiatives across the construction project lifecycle. According to organizers, the partnership will focus on improving safety education, expanding access to training resources, and promoting consistent safety frameworks throughout the industry. The alliance also emphasizes workforce wellbeing and increased stakeholder engagement across all phases of construction projects, from planning and design through operations.'The alliance with OSHA is an important step forward in unifying the industry,' said Adam Jelen, president and CEO of Gilbane Building and chair of Construction Safety Week 2026. 'When industry and government come together, we can drive stronger engagement, awareness, common frameworks, and better education.' This year’s Construction Safety Week theme, 'All in Together,' centered on the principles of 'Recognize, Respond, Respect,' which organizers described as a call to action focused on high-energy and high-hazard work environments where serious injuries and fatalities are most likely to occur.Industry leaders, trade workers, safety professionals, and federal representatives attended the event, underscoring what organizers described as a growing industrywide commitment to strengthening a culture of care and accountability. Jelen noted that the initiative reflects a broader responsibility shared across the built environment sector. 'Construction Safety Week’s vision focuses on unifying and deepening the culture of care, grounded in a deep respect for the skilled craft,' he said. 'This vision comes with a responsibility that spans all team members and across every phase of a project: design, planning, construction, and operations — the entire built environment ecosystem.'As part of this year’s programming, Construction Safety Week also introduced a new technical bulletin series developed by its Technical Committee in collaboration with safety experts, industry leaders, and skilled craft professionals. The bulletins are designed to support more proactive management of high-risk construction hazards and align with the event’s 'Recognize, Respond, Respect' framework. The National Safety Stand-Down to Prevent Falls in Construction remains one of the industry’s largest

+ GILBANE DEVELOPMENT ELEVATES ANDREAS VLAHAKIS TO LEAD CAPITAL MARKETS

Gilbane Development has appointed Andreas Vlahakis as head of capital markets, a move aimed at strengthening the firm’s investment strategies. As of 28th April 2026, Vlahakis will operate from the company’s New York City office, bringing with him a wealth of experience in real estate capital markets across various asset classes, particularly in ground-up multifamily and student housing investments.In his new role at Gilbane Development, Vlahakis will be responsible for refining and executing the firm's funding strategy. Reporting directly to James Patchett, president and CEO, he will play a pivotal role in setting the capital strategy, making crucial investment decisions, and developing strong relationships with funding and equity partners. His expertise will also extend to leading transaction structuring and negotiations, positioning Gilbane to capitalize on emerging market opportunities.Before joining Gilbane Development, Vlahakis served as managing director for capital markets at Thor Equities, where he was instrumental in leading equity-raising efforts for the firm's various projects and partnerships. His prior experience also includes a role as senior vice president and chief of staff at Thor Equities, further underscoring his capability in navigating complex financial landscapes. Vlahakis's extensive background equips him with the insight necessary to enhance Gilbane's standing within the competitive real estate sector.Gilbane Development, known for its commitment to quality and innovation in real estate, is strategically positioning itself for growth with this significant leadership change. By appointing Vlahakis, the firm aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its capital strategies and investment portfolio, particularly in sectors that align with evolving market demands. Stakeholders and investors will be keenly observing how Vlahakis's leadership will influence Gilbane’s future endeavors.As the real estate market continues to evolve, the importance of strong leadership in capital markets becomes increasingly evident. Gilbane Development's decision to bring on Andreas Vlahakis reflects a proactive approach to securing its capital needs and driving forward its ambitious growth plans. Investors will likely be curious to see how Vlahakis's experience will translate into tangible results for the firm.

+ WKU ANNOUNCES GILBANE PARTNERSHIP, PAVING THE WAY FOR NEW DEVELOPMENTS

The Western Kentucky University (WKU) Student Government Association (SGA) has officially announced the approval of WKU's public-private partnership with Gilbane. This significant development indicates the beginning of a collaborative effort aimed at enhancing student facilities and resources. As part of the partnership, which was formally approved by WKU’s Board of Regents on 11th December 2025, Gilbane will design and develop a new residence hall, intended to replace the outdated Douglas Keen and Hugh Poland Halls.Following the recent approval from the Kentucky Legislature on 6th April 2026, WKU is poised to commence construction on the new residence halls. SGA Senator Jakob Barker confirmed that the partnership with Gilbane is now fully authorized, stating, "WKU is now in the clear to start building the new residence halls." This development not only addresses housing needs but also reflects WKU's commitment to improving the overall student experience.In addition to the housing initiative, the SGA has passed several legislative measures aimed at enhancing student resources. Among these is Resolution 7-26-S, which supports the installation of a crosswalk at a crucial intersection near student housing. The resolution highlights the safety concerns of students who currently navigate busy streets to access essential facilities, advocating for collaboration with the Kentucky Department of Transportation for this project.Further addressing financial constraints faced by students, Bill 17-26-S was also approved, establishing the Textbooks for Toppers reimbursement program. This initiative will provide $50 reimbursements for students purchasing required course materials, a move that reflects the SGA's recognition of the financial challenges many students encounter. Additionally, other bills passed will provide essential academic supplies, such as Scantrons and lab goggles, ensuring that students have access to necessary materials without added financial burden.As WKU moves forward with its partnership with Gilbane and implements these new funding initiatives, the SGA's commitment to improving the student experience is clear. The collaboration with Gilbane not only aims to enhance student housing but also signifies a broader strategy to address various student needs and concerns, fostering a supportive academic environment.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLY REINER CACERES JOINS CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP AS HR DIRECTOR

Carly Reiner Caceres has been appointed as the Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, bringing a wealth of experience in people operations and talent strategy. On the 29th of April 2026, Caceres officially began her new role, where she will be instrumental in supporting national campus recruiting initiatives aimed at bolstering early-career talent pipelines for one of the largest building and infrastructure companies in the United States.Prior to her appointment at Clark Construction Group, Caceres served as the Vice President of People Operations at Proper, where she was pivotal in shaping the HR strategy and operational support within a hybrid work environment. Her extensive background includes significant roles at MILLER as the Director of Internal Operations and more than nine years at Accenture, where she excelled in various capacities including Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Caceres’ career trajectory showcases her expertise in full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder management, and employer branding, which will be crucial as Clark Construction Group seeks to attract top talent from universities across the nation. Her previous experience managing campus recruiting strategies at Grant Thornton LLP further solidifies her capabilities in building robust internship programs and enhancing student engagement initiatives.Clark Construction Group, founded in 1906, has established a strong reputation for delivering value across major markets, including education and infrastructure. With a commitment to community strengthening and project excellence, the company is poised to benefit from Caceres' strategic insights and leadership in HR, particularly in nurturing the next generation of professionals in the construction industry.Caceres’ early career involved internships and roles with various organizations, including Charleston Magazine and the Smithsonian Institution’s Hirshhorn Museum, which helped her build a diverse skill set in communications and external relations. As she embarks on this new chapter at Clark Construction Group, her multifaceted background is expected to contribute significantly to the company's growth and talent development objectives.

+ CLARK CONTRACTS SECURES MULTI-TRADES FRAMEWORK WITH RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL

Clark Contracts has been appointed to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades contractors framework, enhancing the delivery of repair, maintenance, and refurbishment services to domestic properties across the local authority area. This announcement was made on 11th February 2026.The framework, which is set to run until 2027, encompasses a variety of multi-trade works. This will enable Renfrewshire Council to effectively manage both responsive and planned maintenance services across its housing stock, ensuring that local residents receive prompt and reliable service.Clark Contracts is already an established player in the public sector, holding several framework agreements that include Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework. Additionally, the company is involved with the Crown Commercial Service’s Construction Framework and various Scottish Procurement Alliance frameworks focusing on Public Buildings, New Build Housing, and more.Gordon Cunningham, managing director at Clark Contracts, expressed enthusiasm about the new appointment, saying, "Building on our strong track record of delivering more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over the past three years, we are pleased to have been appointed to Renfrewshire Council’s framework. As a Paisley-based business, we are proud to be supporting the council and delivering refurbishment and maintenance services that directly benefit local communities."The addition of this framework is expected to bolster Clark Contracts' already significant presence in the public sector, as the company continues to collaborate with various councils and institutions, including the University of Glasgow and Glasgow City Council, to deliver essential services.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP ADVANCES ₱4-B MIXED-USE HUB AT THE VILLAGES

CLARK FREEPORT – A ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination here, has been given the green light with the approval by the Clark Development Corp. of an amendment to PKR Corp.’s lease agreement on 13th December 2025. This ambitious project underscores Clark Construction Group's aggressive strategy to attract significant investments and fortify its reputation as Asia’s Leading Meetings and Conference Destination, a title recently bestowed by the World Travel Awards.The revised lease agreement provides PKR Corp. with a 30-month timeline to complete construction and incorporates stringent performance safeguards to ensure timely project delivery. Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera, president and CEO of Clark Development Corp. (CDC), emphasized that the amendment reflects the organization's commitment to fostering investor confidence and efficient execution of projects. "With this agreement, we ensure that it is a good place for everyone to go and with our coordination, we want to be able to maintain that area [The Villages], an area of preference, where people can reside and really be part of the community," Devanadera stated.PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership with CDC, stating, "We are honored to take part in Clark’s growth and with CDC as our partner, we will deliver this development in Clark." PKR Corp. operates under the umbrella of Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for its expertise in building, civil, residential, and infrastructure projects. The company has received accolades for safety, technology, and workplace practices from various esteemed organizations including the Korea Expressway Corp. and the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy.This mixed-use development is part of CDC’s broader strategy to expedite investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors. The focus on creating integrated lifestyle hubs aligns with the increasing demand for residential options that complement commercial and recreational spaces, positioning Clark as an attractive destination for future developments.Clark Construction Group's proactive approach in securing this project represents a significant step forward in the region's economic landscape, potentially catalyzing further investments and developments. As the construction timeline unfolds, stakeholders will be keenly observing how this initiative contributes to the overall growth and vibrancy of The Villages and the Clark Freeport Zone.
